title: How to get a police certificate – Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) - Canada.ca
content:
## How to get a police certificate – Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC)
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

## Name of the document you need
Extrait du casier judicaire

## Police certificate and IRCC requirements
You must provide a police certificate when you apply for:
- some temporary residence programs
Check your application package or instructions for details.
Apply in person to the Direction Générale de la Police Judiciaire (see Contact information for the complete address).
Apply in person at your nearest Democratic Republic of Congo embassy.
Once the embassy has verified your documents, you must send your request by mail to the Direction Générale de la Police Judiciaire (see Contact information for the complete address).

## Information and documents you need
- A full, certified set of your fingerprints
- 2 passport-style photos
- A birth certificate may be requested
Direction Générale de la Police Judiciaire Direction de l'Identité Judiciaire 14, av. Kalémie, Commune de Gombe Kinshasa, DRC
